QKDpy

API Reference

Top-Level Package

QKDpy: A Python Package for Quantum Key Distribution.

QKDpy is a comprehensive library for Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) simulations, implementing various QKD protocols, quantum simulators, and cryptographic tools.

class qkdpy.QKDConfig(logging: LoggingConfig = <factory>, security: SecurityConfig = <factory>, protocol: ProtocolConfig = <factory>, channel: ChannelConfig = <factory>, ml: MLConfig = <factory>, enterprise: EnterpriseConfig = <factory>, debug_mode: bool = False, strict_validation: bool = True)[source]

Main QKDpy configuration container.

logging: LoggingConfig
security: SecurityConfig
protocol: ProtocolConfig
channel: ChannelConfig
ml: MLConfig
enterprise: EnterpriseConfig
debug_mode: bool = False
strict_validation: bool = True
__init__(logging: LoggingConfig = <factory>, security: SecurityConfig = <factory>, protocol: ProtocolConfig = <factory>, channel: ChannelConfig = <factory>, ml: MLConfig = <factory>, enterprise: EnterpriseConfig = <factory>, debug_mode: bool = False, strict_validation: bool = True) → None
qkdpy.get_config() → QKDConfig[source]

Get the current configuration.

Returns:

Current QKDpy configuration

qkdpy.set_config(config: QKDConfig) → None[source]

Set the global configuration.

If the config includes an enterprise product tier, the licensing module is synchronised automatically.

Parameters:

config – Configuration to set

qkdpy.is_debug_mode() → bool[source]

Check if debug mode is enabled.

qkdpy.is_production_mode() → bool[source]

Check if running in production security mode.

class qkdpy.Qubit(alpha: complex = 1 + 0j, beta: complex = 0j)[source]

Represents a single qubit with state manipulation capabilities.

A qubit state is represented as a complex vector [alpha, beta] where: |psi> = alpha|0> + beta|1> and |alpha|^2 + |beta|^2 = 1

__init__(alpha: complex = 1 + 0j, beta: complex = 0j)[source]

Initialize a qubit with given amplitudes.

Parameters:

  • alpha – Amplitude for |0> state

  • beta – Amplitude for |1> state

Raises:

ValueError – If the state is not normalized

classmethod zero() → Qubit[source]

Create a qubit in the |0> state.

classmethod one() → Qubit[source]

Create a qubit in the |1> state.

classmethod plus() → Qubit[source]

Create a qubit in the |+> state (Hadamard applied to |0>).

classmethod minus() → Qubit[source]

Create a qubit in the |-> state (Hadamard applied to |1>).

property state: ndarray

Get the state vector of the qubit.

property probabilities: tuple[float, float]

Get the probabilities of measuring |0> and |1>.

Returns:

Tuple of (prob_0, prob_1)

apply_gate(gate: ndarray) → None[source]

Apply a quantum gate to the qubit.

Parameters:

gate – 2x2 unitary matrix representing the quantum gate

Raises:

ValueError – If the gate is not a valid 2x2 unitary matrix

measure(basis: str = 'computational') → int[source]

Measure the qubit in the specified basis.

Collapses the qubit’s state to the post-measurement state corresponding to the outcome, as required by quantum mechanics.

Parameters:

basis – ‘computational’ (Z), ‘hadamard’ (X), or ‘circular’ (Y)

Returns:

Measurement result (0 or 1)

collapse_state(result: int, basis: str = 'computational') → None[source]

Collapse the qubit’s state to the measured result in the specified basis.

Parameters:

  • result – The classical measurement result (0 or 1).

  • basis – ‘computational’ (Z), ‘hadamard’ (X), or ‘circular’ (Y).

density_matrix() → ndarray[source]

Calculate the density matrix of the qubit.

Returns:

2x2 density matrix

bloch_vector() → tuple[float, float, float][source]

Calculate the Bloch vector representation of the qubit.

Returns:

Tuple of (x, y, z) coordinates on the Bloch sphere

class qkdpy.Qudit(state: ndarray, dimension: int = 2)[source]

Represents a d-dimensional quantum system (qudit) with state manipulation capabilities.

A qudit state is represented as a complex vector in a d-dimensional Hilbert space: |ψ> = Σ α_i |i> where i ∈ {0, 1, …, d-1} and Σ |α_i|² = 1

__init__(state: ndarray, dimension: int = 2) → None[source]

Initialize a qudit with given state vector.

Parameters:

  • state – Complex state vector of length ‘dimension’

  • dimension – Dimension of the qudit system

Raises:

ValueError – If the state is not properly normalized or has incorrect length

classmethod computational_basis(level: int, dimension: int) → Qudit[source]

Create a qudit in a computational basis state |level>.

Parameters:

  • level – Computational basis level (0 to dimension-1)

  • dimension – Dimension of the qudit system

Returns:

Qudit in the specified computational basis state

classmethod uniform_superposition(dimension: int) → Qudit[source]

Create a qudit in a uniform superposition state.

Parameters:

dimension – Dimension of the qudit system

Returns:

Qudit in uniform superposition state

classmethod fourier_basis(level: int, dimension: int) → Qudit[source]

Create a qudit in a Fourier basis state.

Parameters:

  • level – Fourier basis level (0 to dimension-1)

  • dimension – Dimension of the qudit system

Returns:

Qudit in the specified Fourier basis state

property state: ndarray

Get the state vector of the qudit.

property probabilities: list[float]

Get the probabilities of measuring each computational basis state.

Returns:

List of probabilities for each computational basis state

apply_unitary(operator: ndarray) → None[source]

Apply a unitary operator to the qudit.

Parameters:

operator – d×d unitary matrix representing the quantum operation

Raises:

ValueError – If the operator is not a valid d×d unitary matrix

measure(basis_matrix: ndarray | None = None) → int[source]

Measure the qudit in the specified basis.

Parameters:

basis_matrix – d×d unitary matrix defining the measurement basis. If None, measures in computational basis.

Returns:

Measurement result (integer from 0 to dimension-1)

measure_computational() → int[source]

Measure the qudit in the computational basis.

Returns:

Measurement result (integer from 0 to dimension-1)

measure_fourier() → int[source]

Measure the qudit in the Fourier basis.

Returns:

Measurement result (integer from 0 to dimension-1)

collapse_state(result: int, basis_matrix: ndarray | None = None) → None[source]

Collapse the qudit’s state to a specific measurement result.

Parameters:

  • result – The measurement result (0 to dimension-1)

  • basis_matrix – The measurement basis matrix used, or None for computational basis

fidelity(other_qudit: Qudit) → float[source]

Calculate the fidelity between this qudit and another.

Parameters:

other_qudit – Another qudit to calculate fidelity with

Returns:

Fidelity value between 0 and 1

tensor_product(other: Qudit) → Qudit[source]

Create the tensor product of this qudit with another.

Parameters:

other – Another qudit to tensor with this one

Returns:

New qudit representing the tensor product (has dimension self.dimension * other.dimension)

partial_trace(subsystem_index: int, subsystem_dimension: int) → Qudit[source]

Perform partial trace over a subsystem.

Uses statevector tensordot (same approach as MultiQubitState). Only works for bipartite systems where the result is a pure state (i.e., the original bipartite state is separable across the partition).

Parameters:

  • subsystem_index – Index of the subsystem to trace out (0 or 1)

  • subsystem_dimension – Dimension of the subsystem to trace out

Returns:

Qudit representing the remaining subsystem’s pure state

Raises:

ValueError – If the reduced state is mixed (entangled original state)

class qkdpy.QuantumChannel(distance: float = 1.0, loss_coefficient: float = 0.2, dark_count_rate: float = 1e-06, detector_efficiency: float = 0.1, misalignment_error: float = 0.01, phase_fluctuation_rate: float = 0.05, polarization_drift_rate: float = 0.02, temperature: float = 20.0, eavesdropper: Callable[[Qubit | Qudit], tuple[Qubit | Qudit, bool]] | None = None, loss: float | None = None, noise_model: str = 'none', noise_level: float = 0.0)[source]

Simulates a quantum channel with various noise models and eavesdropping capabilities.

This class allows simulation of quantum channels with different types of noise and potential eavesdropping attacks for QKD protocol analysis.

__init__(distance: float = 1.0, loss_coefficient: float = 0.2, dark_count_rate: float = 1e-06, detector_efficiency: float = 0.1, misalignment_error: float = 0.01, phase_fluctuation_rate: float = 0.05, polarization_drift_rate: float = 0.02, temperature: float = 20.0, eavesdropper: Callable[[Qubit | Qudit], tuple[Qubit | Qudit, bool]] | None = None, loss: float | None = None, noise_model: str = 'none', noise_level: float = 0.0) → None[source]

Initialize a quantum channel with realistic physical properties.

Parameters:

  • distance – Physical distance of the channel in km

  • loss_coefficient – Fiber loss coefficient in dB/km

  • dark_count_rate – Detector dark count rate in Hz

  • detector_efficiency – Photon detector efficiency (0.0 to 1.0)

  • misalignment_error – Probability of basis misalignment

  • phase_fluctuation_rate – Rate of phase fluctuations in the channel

  • polarization_drift_rate – Rate of polarization drift

  • temperature – Temperature in Celsius (affects noise)

  • eavesdropper – Optional function representing an eavesdropping attack

  • loss – Direct loss probability (overrides distance-based calculation)

  • noise_model – Type of noise model (“none”, “depolarizing”, “bit_flip”, etc.)

  • noise_level – Probability/intensity of the noise

transmit(qubit: Qubit | Qudit, timestamp: float = 0.0) → Qubit | Qudit | None[source]

Transmit a qubit through the channel with realistic effects.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to transmit

  • timestamp – Time of transmission (used for temporal effects)

Returns:

The received qubit or None if it was lost

transmit_batch(qubits: list[Qubit | Qudit], start_time: float = 0.0, pulse_interval: float = 1e-09) → list[Qubit | Qudit | None][source]

Transmit a batch of qubits through the channel.

Parameters:

  • qubits – List of qubits to transmit

  • start_time – Starting time for the first qubit

  • pulse_interval – Time interval between qubits (in seconds)

Returns:

List of received qubits (None for lost qubits)

get_statistics() → dict[str, int | float | bool][source]

Get transmission statistics.

Returns:

Dictionary containing transmission statistics

reset_statistics() → None[source]

Reset all statistics counters.

set_eavesdropper(eavesdropper: Callable[[Qubit | Qudit], tuple[Qubit | Qudit, bool]] | None) → None[source]

Set or remove an eavesdropper on the channel.

Parameters:

eavesdropper – Function representing an eavesdropping attack or None to remove

static intercept_resend_attack(qubit: Qubit | Qudit, basis: str = 'random') → tuple[Qubit | Qudit, bool][source]

Implement an intercept-resend eavesdropping attack.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to attack

  • basis – Basis to measure in (‘computational’, ‘hadamard’, ‘circular’, or ‘random’)

Returns:

Tuple of (new qubit, detected) where detected indicates if the attack was detected

static entanglement_attack(qubit: Qubit | Qudit) → tuple[Qubit | Qudit, bool][source]

Implement an entanglement-based eavesdropping attack.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to attack

Returns:

Tuple of (new qubit, detected) where detected indicates if the attack was detected

class qkdpy.QuantumDetector(efficiency: float = 0.1, dark_count_rate: float = 1e-06, dead_time: float = 1e-06, timing_jitter: float = 1e-10, afterpulse_probability: float = 0.01, reset_time: float = 1e-06)[source]

A realistic quantum detector model with physical properties.

This class simulates a quantum detector with realistic properties such as: dark counts, detection efficiency, dead time, timing jitter, and afterpulsing.

__init__(efficiency: float = 0.1, dark_count_rate: float = 1e-06, dead_time: float = 1e-06, timing_jitter: float = 1e-10, afterpulse_probability: float = 0.01, reset_time: float = 1e-06) → None[source]

Initialize the quantum detector.

Parameters:

  • efficiency – Detection efficiency (0.0 to 1.0)

  • dark_count_rate – Rate of dark count events per second

  • dead_time – Time after a detection during which detector is insensitive (s)

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing measurement uncertainty (s)

  • afterpulse_probability – Probability of afterpulsing events

  • reset_time – Time needed to reset the detector after detection (s)

detect(photon_present: bool, timestamp: float, basis: str = 'computational') → tuple[int | None, float][source]

Detect a photon with realistic behavior.

Parameters:

  • photon_present – Whether a photon is present at the detector input

  • timestamp – Time of photon arrival

  • basis – Measurement basis

Returns:

Tuple of (measurement_result, detection_time) where measurement_result is None if no detection occurs, otherwise 0 or 1

measure_state(qubit: Qubit, basis: str = 'computational', timestamp: float = 0.0) → tuple[int | None, float][source]

Measure a qubit state using the detector model.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to measure

  • basis – Measurement basis (‘computational’, ‘hadamard’, ‘circular’)

  • timestamp – Time of measurement

Returns:

Tuple of (measurement_result, detection_time)

get_statistics() → dict[str, Any][source]

Get detector statistics.

Returns:

Dictionary with detector statistics

reset() → None[source]

Reset detector statistics.

class qkdpy.DetectorArray(num_detectors: int = 2, **detector_kwargs: Any)[source]

Array of quantum detectors for multi-basis measurements.

__init__(num_detectors: int = 2, **detector_kwargs: Any) → None[source]

Initialize an array of detectors.

Parameters:

  • num_detectors – Number of detectors in the array

  • **detector_kwargs – Arguments to pass to individual detector constructors

measure_in_basis(qubit: Qubit, basis: str = 'computational', timestamp: float = 0.0) → int[source]

Measure a qubit in a specific basis using the detector array.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to measure

  • basis – Measurement basis

  • timestamp – Time of measurement

Returns:

Measurement result (0 or 1)

get_statistics() → list[dict[str, Any]][source]

Get statistics for all detectors.

Returns:

List of dictionaries with statistics for each detector

class qkdpy.TimingSynchronizer(clock_frequency: float = 1000000000.0, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, synchronization_accuracy: float = 1e-09, max_drift_rate: float = 1e-06)[source]

A realistic timing synchronization model for QKD systems.

__init__(clock_frequency: float = 1000000000.0, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, synchronization_accuracy: float = 1e-09, max_drift_rate: float = 1e-06)[source]

Initialize timing synchronizer.

Parameters:

  • clock_frequency – System clock frequency in Hz

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing jitter in seconds

  • synchronization_accuracy – Accuracy of synchronization in seconds

  • max_drift_rate – Maximum clock drift rate (fraction per second)

synchronize_clocks(synchronization_time: float) → dict[str, float][source]

Perform clock synchronization between Alice and Bob.

Parameters:

synchronization_time – Time of synchronization attempt

Returns:

Dictionary with synchronization results

get_alice_time(reference_time: float) → float[source]

Get Alice’s local time based on reference time and drift.

Parameters:

reference_time – Reference time

Returns:

Alice’s local time

get_bob_time(reference_time: float) → float[source]

Get Bob’s local time based on reference time and drift.

Parameters:

reference_time – Reference time

Returns:

Bob’s local time

calculate_time_difference(reference_time: float) → float[source]

Calculate the time difference between Alice and Bob’s clocks.

Parameters:

reference_time – Reference time for calculation

Returns:

Time difference (Alice’s time - Bob’s time)

update_clock_drift() → None[source]

Update clock drift values (to simulate gradual changes).

class qkdpy.PhotonTimingModel(fiber_length: float, speed_of_light_factor: float = 0.2, detector_timing_resolution: float = 5e-12, source_jitter: float = 2e-12)[source]

Model for photon timing in QKD systems.

__init__(fiber_length: float, speed_of_light_factor: float = 0.2, detector_timing_resolution: float = 5e-12, source_jitter: float = 2e-12)[source]

Initialize photon timing model.

Parameters:

  • fiber_length – Length of optical fiber in meters

  • speed_of_light_factor – Effective speed as fraction of c in fiber

  • detector_timing_resolution – Timing resolution of detectors in seconds

  • source_jitter – Jitter from photon source in seconds

emit_photon(emission_time: float) → float[source]

Simulate photon emission with timing jitter.

Parameters:

emission_time – Desired emission time

Returns:

Actual emission time with jitter

detect_photon(arrival_time: float) → float[source]

Simulate photon detection with timing resolution.

Parameters:

arrival_time – Actual arrival time of photon

Returns:

Measured arrival time with resolution effects

photon_transit_time() → float[source]

Get the transit time for a photon through the fiber.

Returns:

Photon transit time in seconds

class qkdpy.QBERTimingAnalysis(timing_window: float = 1e-09, max_temporal_drift: float = 1e-12)[source]

Analyze timing-dependent QBER in QKD systems.

__init__(timing_window: float = 1e-09, max_temporal_drift: float = 1e-12)[source]

Initialize timing-dependent QBER analysis.

Parameters:

  • timing_window – Time window for matching photon pairs (s)

  • max_temporal_drift – Maximum temporal drift per photon (s)

calculate_temporal_qber(alice_times: list[float], bob_times: list[float], expected_delay: float = 0.0) → float[source]

Calculate QBER based on timing mismatches.

Parameters:

  • alice_times – List of photon emission times from Alice

  • bob_times – List of photon detection times at Bob

  • expected_delay – Expected delay between Alice and Bob

Returns:

Estimated QBER due to timing mismatches

update_with_drift(drift_rate: float, time_elapsed: float) → float[source]

Update timing window based on clock drift.

Parameters:

  • drift_rate – Rate of temporal drift (s/s)

  • time_elapsed – Time since last synchronization (s)

Returns:

Adjusted timing window to account for drift

class qkdpy.ProtocolTimingManager(synchronizer: TimingSynchronizer, photon_model: PhotonTimingModel, qber_analyzer: QBERTimingAnalysis)[source]

Manage timing aspects of QKD protocols.

__init__(synchronizer: TimingSynchronizer, photon_model: PhotonTimingModel, qber_analyzer: QBERTimingAnalysis)[source]

Initialize protocol timing manager.

Parameters:

  • synchronizer – Timing synchronizer instance

  • photon_model – Photon timing model instance

  • qber_analyzer – QBER timing analysis instance

send_photon_sequence(start_time: float, pulse_interval: float, num_photons: int, basis_sequence: list[str]) → tuple[list[float], list[str]][source]

Simulate sending a sequence of photons with proper timing.

Parameters:

  • start_time – Start time for sequence

  • pulse_interval – Interval between pulses

  • num_photons – Number of photons to send

  • basis_sequence – Sequence of bases to encode

Returns:

Tuple of (emission_times, basis_sequence)

receive_photon_sequence(emission_times: list[float], expected_delay: float, basis_sequence: list[str]) → tuple[list[float], list[str], float][source]

Simulate receiving a sequence of photons with proper timing.

Parameters:

  • emission_times – List of photon emission times from Alice

  • expected_delay – Expected delay for photons

  • basis_sequence – Sequence of bases that were encoded

Returns:

Tuple of (detection_times, measurement_bases, timing_qber)

get_timing_stats() → dict[source]

Get statistics about timing performance.

Returns:

Dictionary with timing statistics

class qkdpy.PhotonSource(name: str = 'Photon Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1)[source]

Base class for realistic photon sources in QKD systems.

__init__(name: str = 'Photon Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1)[source]

Initialize a basic photon source.

Parameters:

  • name – Name of the photon source

  • pulse_rate – Rate of photon pulse generation (Hz)

  • wavelength – Wavelength of the photons (m)

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing jitter (s)

  • power – Average optical power in watts

  • efficiency – Coupling efficiency to the quantum channel

generate_photon_pulse(time: float) → tuple[Any, float] | tuple[PhotonSourceState, float, str] | tuple[bool, float, dict[str, bool]][source]

Generate a photon pulse at the specified time.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_present, actual_time_with_jitter)

class qkdpy.WeakCoherentSource(name: str = 'Weak Coherent Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, mean_photon_number: float = 0.1, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1)[source]

Model for weak coherent photon sources used in QKD.

__init__(name: str = 'Weak Coherent Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, mean_photon_number: float = 0.1, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1)[source]

Initialize a weak coherent source.

Parameters:

  • name – Name of the photon source

  • pulse_rate – Rate of photon pulse generation (Hz)

  • wavelength – Wavelength of the photons (m)

  • mean_photon_number – Average number of photons per pulse

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing jitter (s)

  • power – Average optical power in watts

  • efficiency – Coupling efficiency to the quantum channel

generate_photon_pulse(time: float) → tuple[PhotonSourceState, float][source]

Generate a photon pulse from the weak coherent source.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_state, actual_time_with_jitter)

get_photon_statistics(num_pulses: int = 1000) → dict[str, float][source]

Get photon statistics for the source.

Parameters:

num_pulses – Number of pulses to simulate

Returns:

Dictionary with photon statistics

class qkdpy.DecoyStateSource(name: str = 'Decoy State Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, signal_mean_photon_number: float = 0.5, decoy_mean_photon_numbers: list[float] | None = None, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1, decoy_probability: float = 0.1, random_number_generator: Generator | None = None)[source]

Model for decoy state photon sources used to enhance security.

__init__(name: str = 'Decoy State Source', pulse_rate: float = 1000000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, signal_mean_photon_number: float = 0.5, decoy_mean_photon_numbers: list[float] | None = None, timing_jitter: float = 1e-12, power: float = 1e-06, efficiency: float = 0.1, decoy_probability: float = 0.1, random_number_generator: Generator | None = None)[source]

Initialize a decoy state source.

Parameters:

  • name – Name of the photon source

  • pulse_rate – Rate of photon pulse generation (Hz)

  • wavelength – Wavelength of the photons (m)

  • signal_mean_photon_number – Average photons for signal pulses

  • decoy_mean_photon_numbers – List of average photons for decoy pulses

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing jitter (s)

  • power – Average optical power in watts

  • efficiency – Coupling efficiency to the quantum channel

  • decoy_probability – Probability of sending a decoy pulse

  • random_number_generator – Random number generator instance

generate_signal_pulse(time: float) → tuple[PhotonSourceState, float, str][source]

Generate a signal photon pulse.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_state, actual_time_with_jitter, pulse_type)

generate_decoy_pulse(time: float) → tuple[PhotonSourceState, float, str][source]

Generate a decoy photon pulse.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_state, actual_time_with_jitter, pulse_type)

generate_photon_pulse(time: float) → tuple[PhotonSourceState, float, str][source]

Generate a photon pulse, randomly selecting signal or decoy state.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_state, actual_time_with_jitter, pulse_type)

get_pulse_type_statistics(num_pulses: int = 1000) → dict[str, Any][source]

Get statistics about pulse types generated.

Parameters:

num_pulses – Number of pulses to simulate

Returns:

Dictionary with pulse type statistics

class qkdpy.ParametricDownConversionSource(name: str = 'PDC Source', pulse_rate: float = 100000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, pair_generation_rate: float = 1000000.0, timing_jitter: float = 2e-12, efficiency: float = 0.05, heralding_efficiency: float = 0.15, spectral_width: float = 1000000000000.0)[source]

Model for parametric down conversion photon pair sources.

__init__(name: str = 'PDC Source', pulse_rate: float = 100000000.0, wavelength: float = 1.55e-06, pair_generation_rate: float = 1000000.0, timing_jitter: float = 2e-12, efficiency: float = 0.05, heralding_efficiency: float = 0.15, spectral_width: float = 1000000000000.0)[source]

Initialize a parametric down conversion source.

Parameters:

  • name – Name of the photon source

  • pulse_rate – Rate of pump pulses (Hz)

  • wavelength – Wavelength of the down-converted photons (m)

  • pair_generation_rate – Rate of photon pair generation (pairs/s)

  • timing_jitter – Standard deviation of timing jitter (s)

  • efficiency – Overall efficiency of the source

  • heralding_efficiency – Efficiency of detecting one photon to herald the other

  • spectral_width – Spectral width of the photons (FWHM) in Hz

generate_photon_pulse(time: float) → tuple[bool, float, dict[str, bool]][source]

Generate a photon pulse from the PDC source.

Parameters:

time – Time of pulse generation

Returns:

Tuple of (photon_present, actual_time_with_jitter, additional_info)

class qkdpy.PhotonSourceManager[source]

Manager for coordinating multiple photon sources in QKD protocols.

__init__() → None[source]

Initialize the photon source manager.

add_source(source_id: str, source: PhotonSource) → None[source]

Add a photon source to the manager.

Parameters:

  • source_id – Unique identifier for the source

  • source – PhotonSource instance to add

set_active_source(source_id: str) → None[source]

Set the active photon source.

Parameters:

source_id – ID of the source to make active

generate_sequence(duration: float, source_id: str | None = None, timestamps: list[float] | None = None) → list[tuple[Any, float] | tuple[PhotonSourceState, float, str] | tuple[bool, float, dict[str, bool]]][source]

Generate a sequence of photon pulses.

Parameters:

  • duration – Duration of sequence in seconds

  • source_id – ID of source to use (default: active source)

  • timestamps – Specific timestamps for pulse generation (optional)

Returns:

List of (photon_state, time) tuples

class qkdpy.SecurityAnalyzer[source]

Comprehensive security analysis tools for QKD protocols.

__init__() → None[source]

Initialize the security analyzer.

perform_security_analysis(protocol_name: str, qber: float, key_rate: float, channel_loss: float, mean_photon_number: float, num_decoy_states: int = 0) → dict[str, Any][source]

Perform comprehensive security analysis for a QKD protocol.

Parameters:

  • protocol_name – Name of the QKD protocol

  • qber – Quantum Bit Error Rate

  • key_rate – Raw key generation rate

  • channel_loss – Channel loss in dB

  • mean_photon_number – Mean photon number per pulse

  • num_decoy_states – Number of decoy states used (0 for no decoys)

Returns:

Dictionary with comprehensive security analysis

simulate_attack(attack_type: AttackType, protocol_name: str, original_qber: float, channel_loss: float, mean_photon_number: float) → dict[str, Any][source]

Simulate the effects of a specific attack on the protocol.

Parameters:

  • attack_type – Type of attack to simulate

  • protocol_name – Name of the protocol

  • original_qber – Original QBER without attack

  • channel_loss – Channel loss in dB

  • mean_photon_number – Mean photon number per pulse

Returns:

Dictionary with attack simulation results

class qkdpy.QBERAnalysis[source]

Analysis tools specifically for QBER (Quantum Bit Error Rate).

__init__() → None[source]

Initialize QBER analysis tools.

analyze_qber_trends(qber_values: list[float], time_intervals: list[float] | None = None, window_size: int = 10) → dict[str, Any][source]

Analyze trends in QBER values over time.

Parameters:

  • qber_values – List of QBER values over time

  • time_intervals – Optional time intervals for each QBER value

  • window_size – Size of sliding window for trend analysis

Returns:

Dictionary with QBER trend analysis

class qkdpy.SideChannelAnalyzer[source]

Analyzer for side-channel attacks in QKD systems.

__init__() → None[source]

Initialize side-channel analyzer.

analyze_detector_side_channels(detector_timing: list[tuple[float, bool]], detector_settings: list[dict[str, float]]) → dict[str, Any][source]

Analyze potential side-channel vulnerabilities in detectors.

Parameters:

  • detector_timing – List of (timestamp, detection_occurred) tuples

  • detector_settings – List of detector configuration parameters

Returns:

Dictionary with side-channel analysis

class qkdpy.AttackType(value)[source]

Types of attacks that can be simulated in QKD protocols.

INTERCEPT_RESEND = 'intercept-resend'
PHASE_CLONE = 'phase-cloning'
BEAM_SPLITTING = 'beam-splitting'
FAKED_STATE = 'faked-state'
TIME_SHIFT = 'time-shift'
SPLIT_AND_DELAY = 'split-and-delay'
COLLECTOR = 'collector'
class qkdpy.QuantumGate(matrix: ndarray)[source]

Base class for quantum gates.

__init__(matrix: ndarray)[source]
property matrix: ndarray
class qkdpy.ExtendedQuantumChannel(loss: float = 0.0, noise_model: str = 'depolarizing', noise_level: float = 0.0, eavesdropper: Callable | None = None)[source]

Extended quantum channel with additional noise models.

__init__(loss: float = 0.0, noise_model: str = 'depolarizing', noise_level: float = 0.0, eavesdropper: Callable | None = None) → None[source]

Initialize an extended quantum channel.

Parameters:

  • loss – Probability of losing a qubit in the channel (0.0 to 1.0)

  • noise_model – Type of noise (‘depolarizing’, ‘bit_flip’, ‘phase_flip’, ‘amplitude_damping’, ‘phase_damping’, ‘generalized_amplitude_damping’)

  • noise_level – Intensity of the noise (0.0 to 1.0)

  • eavesdropper – Optional function representing an eavesdropping attack

transmit(qubit: Qubit) → Qubit | None[source]

Transmit a qubit through the channel.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to transmit

Returns:

The received qubit or None if it was lost

get_statistics() → dict[source]

Get transmission statistics.

Returns:

Dictionary containing transmission statistics

reset_statistics() → None[source]

Reset all statistics counters.

set_eavesdropper(eavesdropper: Callable | None) → None[source]

Set or remove an eavesdropper on the channel.

Parameters:

eavesdropper – Function representing an eavesdropping attack or None to remove

class qkdpy.MultiQubitState(state: ndarray)[source]

Represents a multi-qubit quantum state.

A multi-qubit state is represented as a complex vector in a 2^n dimensional Hilbert space where n is the number of qubits.

__init__(state: ndarray)[source]

Initialize a multi-qubit state with a given state vector.

Parameters:

state – Complex vector representing the quantum state

Raises:

ValueError – If the state dimension is not a power of 2

classmethod from_qubits(qubits: list[Qubit]) → MultiQubitState[source]

Create a multi-qubit state from individual qubits.

Parameters:

qubits – List of Qubit objects

Returns:

MultiQubitState representing the tensor product of the qubits

classmethod zeros(num_qubits: int) → MultiQubitState[source]

Create a multi-qubit state |00...0>.

Parameters:

num_qubits – Number of qubits

Returns:

MultiQubitState in the all-zeros state

classmethod ghz(num_qubits: int) → MultiQubitState[source]

Create a GHZ (Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger) state.

The GHZ state is (|00...0> + |11...1>) / sqrt(2)

Parameters:

num_qubits – Number of qubits

Returns:

MultiQubitState in the GHZ state

classmethod w_state(num_qubits: int) → MultiQubitState[source]

Create a W state.

The W state is (|100...0> + |010...0> + ... + |000...1>) / sqrt(n)

Parameters:

num_qubits – Number of qubits

Returns:

MultiQubitState in the W state

property num_qubits: int

Get the number of qubits in the state.

property state: ndarray

Get the state vector.

property probabilities: ndarray

Get the probabilities of measuring each computational basis state.

Returns:

Array of probabilities for each of the 2^n basis states

apply_gate(gate: ndarray, target_qubits: int | list[int]) → None[source]

Apply a quantum gate to specific qubits.

Parameters:

  • gate – Unitary matrix representing the quantum gate

  • target_qubits – Index or list of indices of qubits to apply the gate to

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the gate size doesn’t match the number of target qubits

  • ValueError – If target qubit indices are invalid

measure(target_qubit: int) → tuple[int, MultiQubitState | None][source]

Measure a specific qubit in the computational basis.

Parameters:

target_qubit – Index of the qubit to measure

Returns:

Tuple of (measurement_result, collapsed_state) where collapsed_state is the state of the remaining qubits (None if this was the last qubit)

Raises:

ValueError – If target_qubit is invalid

density_matrix() → ndarray[source]

Calculate the density matrix of the multi-qubit state.

Returns:

2^n x 2^n density matrix

entanglement_entropy(subsystem_qubits: list[int]) → float[source]

Calculate the entanglement entropy of a subsystem.

Computes S(rho_reduced) = -Tr(rho_reduced * log2(rho_reduced)) where rho_reduced is obtained by tracing out all qubits NOT in subsystem_qubits.

Parameters:

subsystem_qubits – List of qubit indices in the subsystem

Returns:

Von Neumann entropy of the subsystem (base-2)

fidelity(other: MultiQubitState) → float[source]

Calculate the fidelity between this state and another state.

Parameters:

other – Another MultiQubitState

Returns:

Fidelity value between 0 and 1

Raises:

ValueError – If the states have different numbers of qubits

class qkdpy.Measurement[source]

Provides various quantum measurement operations for QKD protocols.

This class implements different measurement bases and strategies used in quantum key distribution protocols.

static measure_in_basis(qubit: Qubit | Qudit, basis: str = 'computational') → int[source]

Measure a qubit/qudit in the specified basis.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit/qudit to measure

  • basis – Measurement basis (‘computational’, ‘hadamard’, ‘circular’, ‘fourier’)

Returns:

Measurement result (integer)

Raises:

ValueError – If an unsupported basis is specified for Qudit

static measure_batch_in_basis(qubits: list[Qubit | Qudit], basis: str = 'computational') → list[int][source]

Measure a batch of qubits/qudits in the specified basis.

Parameters:

  • qubits – List of qubits/qudits to measure

  • basis – Measurement basis (‘computational’, ‘hadamard’, ‘circular’, ‘fourier’)

Returns:

List of measurement results (integer for each quantum state)

static measure_in_random_basis(qubit: Qubit | Qudit, bases: list[str] | None = None) → tuple[int, str][source]

Measure a qubit/qudit in a randomly chosen basis.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit/qudit to measure

  • bases – List of bases to choose from (default: [‘computational’, ‘hadamard’])

Returns:

Tuple of (measurement result, chosen basis)

static measure_batch_in_random_bases(qubits: list[Qubit | Qudit], bases: list[str] | None = None) → tuple[list[int], list[str]][source]

Measure a batch of qubits/qudits in randomly chosen bases.

Parameters:

  • qubits – List of qubits/qudits to measure

  • bases – List of bases to choose from (default: [‘computational’, ‘hadamard’])

Returns:

Tuple of (measurement results, chosen bases)

static measure_state_fidelity(qubit: Qubit, target_state: ndarray) → float[source]

Measure the fidelity between a qubit state and a target state.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to measure

  • target_state – The target state vector

Returns:

Fidelity value between 0 and 1

static measure_bloch_coordinates(qubit: Qubit) → tuple[float, float, float][source]

Measure the Bloch sphere coordinates of a qubit.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to measure

Returns:

Tuple of (x, y, z) coordinates on the Bloch sphere

static measure_density_matrix(qubit: Qubit) → ndarray[source]

Measure the density matrix of a qubit.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to measure

Returns:

2x2 density matrix

static measure_purity(qubit: Qubit) → float[source]

Measure the purity of a qubit state.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to measure

Returns:

Purity value (1 for pure states, <1 for mixed states)

static measure_von_neumann_entropy(qubit: Qubit) → float[source]

Measure the von Neumann entropy of a qubit state.

Parameters:

qubit – The qubit to measure

Returns:

Von Neumann entropy value

static measure_observable(qubit: Qubit, observable: ndarray) → float[source]

Measure the expectation value of an observable.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to measure

  • observable – Hermitian matrix representing the observable

Returns:

Expectation value of the observable

Raises:

ValueError – If the observable is not Hermitian

static measure_bell_state(qubit1: Qubit, qubit2: Qubit) → str[source]

Measure which Bell state two qubits are in.

Parameters:

  • qubit1 – First qubit

  • qubit2 – Second qubit

Returns:

String representing the Bell state (‘Φ+’, ‘Φ-’, ‘Ψ+’, ‘Ψ-‘)

static quantum_state_tomography(qubit: Qubit | Qudit, num_measurements: int = 1000) → dict[str, float][source]

Perform quantum state tomography to reconstruct the density matrix.

Parameters:

  • qubit – The qubit to measure

  • num_measurements – Number of measurements to perform for each basis

Returns:

Dictionary containing the reconstructed density matrix elements

Phase gate (S gate).

SWAP gate.

class qkdpy.GateUtils[source]
static basis_switch(basis: str) → ndarray[source]

Get a gate to switch to a specific measurement basis.

Parameters:

basis – ‘computational’, ‘hadamard’, or ‘circular’

Returns:

2x2 unitary matrix for basis switch

Raises:

ValueError – If the basis is not recognized

static random_unitary() → ndarray[source]

Generate a random unitary matrix.

Returns:

2x2 random unitary matrix

static unitary_from_angles(theta: float, phi: float, lam: float) → ndarray[source]

Create a unitary matrix from Euler angles.

Parameters:

  • theta – First rotation angle

  • phi – Second rotation angle

  • lam – Third rotation angle

Returns:

2x2 unitary matrix

static sequence(*gates: ndarray) → ndarray[source]

Compose multiple gates in sequence.

Parameters:

*gates – Variable number of gates to apply in sequence

Returns:

Combined unitary matrix

static tensor_product(*gates: ndarray) → ndarray[source]

Compute the tensor product of multiple gates.

Parameters:

*gates – Variable number of gates

Returns:

Tensor product of all gates

static is_unitary(gate: ndarray, tol: float = 1e-10) → bool[source]

Check if a matrix is unitary.

Parameters:

  • gate – Matrix to check

  • tol – Tolerance for the unitarity check

Returns:

True if the matrix is unitary, False otherwise

static is_hermitian(gate: ndarray, tol: float = 1e-10) → bool[source]

Check if a matrix is Hermitian.

Parameters:

  • gate – Matrix to check

  • tol – Tolerance for the Hermitian check

Returns:

True if the matrix is Hermitian, False otherwise

class qkdpy.OneTimePad[source]

One-time pad encryption using quantum keys.

This class implements the one-time pad encryption scheme, which is information-theoretically secure when used with a truly random key that is at least as long as the message and never reused.

static encrypt(message: str, key: list[int]) → tuple[list[int], list[int]][source]

Encrypt a message using the one-time pad.

Parameters:

  • message – Message to encrypt

  • key – Binary key for encryption

Returns:

Tuple of (ciphertext, remaining_key)

Raises:

ValueError – If the key is shorter than the message

static encrypt_file(file_path: str, key: list[int], output_path: str | None = None) → tuple[str, list[int]][source]

Encrypt a file using the one-time pad.

Parameters:

  • file_path – Path to the file to encrypt

  • key – Binary key for encryption

  • output_path – Path to save the encrypted file (optional)

Returns:

Tuple of (output_path, remaining_key)

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the key is shorter than the file

  • FileNotFoundError – If the input file does not exist

static decrypt(ciphertext: list[int], key: list[int]) → str

Decrypt a message using the one-time pad.

Parameters:

  • ciphertext – Ciphertext to decrypt

  • key – Binary key for decryption

Returns:

Decrypted message

Raises:

ValueError – If the key is shorter than the ciphertext

static decrypt_file(file_path: str, key: list[int], output_path: str | None = None) → str

Decrypt a file using the one-time pad.

Parameters:

  • file_path – Path to the file to decrypt

  • key – Binary key for decryption

  • output_path – Path to save the decrypted file (optional)

Returns:

Path to the decrypted file

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the key is shorter than the file

  • FileNotFoundError – If the input file does not exist

class qkdpy.QuantumAuth[source]

Authentication using quantum keys.

This class provides authentication methods that can be used with quantum keys to ensure the integrity and authenticity of messages.

static generate_mac(message: str, key: list[int], hash_algorithm: str = 'sha256') → str[source]

Generate a Message Authentication Code (MAC) for a message.

Parameters:

  • message – Message to authenticate

  • key – Binary key for authentication

  • hash_algorithm – Hash algorithm to use (‘sha256’, ‘sha512’, etc.)

Returns:

MAC as a hexadecimal string

static verify_mac(message: str, mac: str, key: list[int], hash_algorithm: str = 'sha256') → bool[source]

Verify a Message Authentication Code (MAC) for a message.

Parameters:

  • message – Message to verify

  • mac – MAC to verify against

  • key – Binary key for authentication

  • hash_algorithm – Hash algorithm to use (‘sha256’, ‘sha512’, etc.)

Returns:

True if the MAC is valid, False otherwise

static generate_authenticator(key: list[int], challenge: str | None = None) → str[source]

Generate an authenticator for challenge-response authentication.

Parameters:

  • key – Binary key for authentication

  • challenge – Challenge string (optional)

Returns:

Authenticator as a hexadecimal string

static verify_authenticator(key: list[int], challenge: str, authenticator: str) → bool[source]

Verify an authenticator for challenge-response authentication.

Parameters:

  • key – Binary key for authentication

  • challenge – Challenge string

  • authenticator – Authenticator to verify

Returns:

True if the authenticator is valid, False otherwise

static generate_key_fingerprint(key: list[int], hash_algorithm: str = 'sha256') → str[source]

Generate a fingerprint for a key.

Parameters:

  • key – Binary key to fingerprint

  • hash_algorithm – Hash algorithm to use (‘sha256’, ‘sha512’, etc.)

Returns:

Key fingerprint as a hexadecimal string

static generate_commitment(value: str, key: list[int], nonce: int | None = None) → dict[str, str][source]

Generate a cryptographic commitment for a value.

Parameters:

  • value – Value to commit to

  • key – Binary key for the commitment

  • nonce – Nonce for the commitment (optional)

Returns:

Dictionary containing the commitment and nonce

static verify_commitment(value: str, commitment: str, key: list[int], nonce: str) → bool[source]

Verify a cryptographic commitment for a value.

Parameters:

  • value – Value to verify

  • commitment – Commitment to verify against

  • key – Binary key for the commitment

  • nonce – Nonce used for the commitment

Returns:

True if the commitment is valid, False otherwise

class qkdpy.QuantumAuthenticator(channel: QuantumChannel)[source]

Provides quantum-based authentication mechanisms.

__init__(channel: QuantumChannel)[source]

Initialize the quantum authenticator.

Parameters:

channel – Quantum channel for authentication protocol

register_party(party_id: str, shared_key_length: int = 128) → bool[source]

Register a party for quantum authentication.

Parameters:

  • party_id – Unique identifier for the party

  • shared_key_length – Length of the shared key to generate

Returns:

True if registration successful, False otherwise

authenticate_party(party_id: str, challenge: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Authenticate a registered party.

Parameters:

  • party_id – Unique identifier for the party

  • challenge – Challenge string (optional)

Returns:

Authentication token if successful, None otherwise

verify_authentication(party_id: str, token_id: str, challenge: str) → bool[source]

Verify an authentication token.

Parameters:

  • party_id – Unique identifier for the party

  • token_id – Authentication token identifier

  • challenge – Challenge string

Returns:

True if authentication is valid, False otherwise

generate_quantum_signature(party_id: str, message: str) → tuple[str, str] | None[source]

Generate a quantum digital signature for a message.

Parameters:

  • party_id – Unique identifier for the signing party

  • message – Message to sign

Returns:

Tuple of (signature, timestamp) if successful, None otherwise

verify_quantum_signature(party_id: str, message: str, signature: str, timestamp: str) → bool[source]

Verify a quantum digital signature.

Parameters:

  • party_id – Unique identifier for the signing party

  • message – Message that was signed

  • signature – Signature to verify

  • timestamp – Timestamp of the signature

Returns:

True if signature is valid, False otherwise

get_party_info(party_id: str) → dict | None[source]

Get information about an authenticated party.

Parameters:

party_id – Unique identifier for the party

Returns:

Dictionary with party information, None if not found

remove_party(party_id: str) → bool[source]

Remove an authenticated party.

Parameters:

party_id – Unique identifier for the party

Returns:

True if successful, False otherwise

class qkdpy.QuantumKeyExchange(channel: QuantumChannel)[source]

Manages quantum key exchange between parties.

__init__(channel: QuantumChannel)[source]

Initialize the quantum key exchange system.

Parameters:

channel – Quantum channel for key exchange

initiate_key_exchange(party_a: str, party_b: str, key_length: int = 128, protocol: str = 'BB84', timeout: float = 30.0) → str | None[source]

Initiate a quantum key exchange between two parties.

Parameters:

  • party_a – Identifier for the first party

  • party_b – Identifier for the second party

  • key_length – Desired length of the exchanged key

  • protocol – QKD protocol to use (‘BB84’, ‘E91’, etc.)

  • timeout – Maximum time to wait for exchange completion

Returns:

Session identifier if successful, None otherwise

execute_key_exchange(session_id: str) → bool[source]

Execute the quantum key exchange for a session.

Parameters:

session_id – Session identifier

Returns:

True if exchange successful, False otherwise

get_shared_key(session_id: str) → list[int] | None[source]

Get the shared key from a completed exchange.

Parameters:

session_id – Session identifier

Returns:

Shared key if exchange was successful, None otherwise

verify_key_exchange(session_id: str, party: str, challenge: str | None = None) → str | None[source]

Verify a party’s participation in a key exchange.

Parameters:

  • session_id – Session identifier

  • party – Party identifier

  • challenge – Challenge string for authentication (optional)

Returns:

Authentication token if verification successful, None otherwise

get_session_info(session_id: str) → dict[str, Any] | None[source]

Get information about a key exchange session.

Parameters:

session_id – Session identifier

Returns:

Dictionary with session information, None if not found

get_exchange_statistics() → dict[str, Any][source]

Get statistics about key exchanges.

Returns:

Dictionary with exchange statistics

cleanup_expired_sessions(max_age: float = 3600.0) → int[source]

Remove expired sessions.

Parameters:

max_age – Maximum age of sessions in seconds

Returns:

Number of sessions cleaned up

export_session_log(filename: str) → bool[source]

Export session log to a file.

Parameters:

filename – Name of the file to export to

Returns:

True if successful, False otherwise

rotate_key(session_id: str, new_key_length: int | None = None) → bool[source]

Rotate the key for an active session.

Parameters:

  • session_id – Session identifier

  • new_key_length – Length of the new key (optional, defaults to current key length)

Returns:

True if rotation successful, False otherwise

class qkdpy.QuantumRandomNumberGenerator(channel: QuantumChannel | None = None)[source]

Generates cryptographically secure random numbers using quantum principles.

__init__(channel: QuantumChannel | None = None)[source]

Initialize the quantum random number generator.

Parameters:

channel – Quantum channel for QKD-based randomness (optional)

generate_random_bits(num_bits: int) → list[int][source]

Generate cryptographically secure random bits.

Parameters:

num_bits – Number of random bits to generate

Returns:

List of random bits

generate_random_bytes(num_bytes: int) → bytes[source]

Generate cryptographically secure random bytes.

Parameters:

num_bytes – Number of random bytes to generate

Returns:

Random bytes

generate_random_int(min_val: int, max_val: int) → int[source]

Generate a cryptographically secure random integer in a range.

Parameters:

  • min_val – Minimum value (inclusive)

  • max_val – Maximum value (inclusive)

Returns:

Random integer in the specified range

generate_random_string(length: int, charset: str = 'alphanumeric') → str[source]

Generate a cryptographically secure random string.

Parameters:

  • length – Length of the string to generate

  • charset – Character set to use (‘alphanumeric’, ‘hex’, ‘binary’, or custom)

Returns:

Random string

add_entropy(entropy_source: list[int]) → None[source]

Add external entropy to the pool.

Parameters:

entropy_source – List of random bits to add to the entropy pool

get_entropy_level() → float[source]

Estimate the entropy level in the pool.

Returns:

Estimated entropy level (0.0 to 1.0)

calibrate() → bool[source]

Calibrate the random number generator.

Returns:

True if calibration successful, False otherwise

get_statistics() → dict[source]

Get statistics about the random number generator.

Returns:

Dictionary with generator statistics

class qkdpy.QuantumAuthentication[source]

Quantum authentication protocols for enhanced security.

static generate_message_authentication_code(key: list[int], message: bytes, algorithm: str = 'sha256') → str[source]

Generate a message authentication code using a quantum key.

Parameters:

  • key – Quantum key as a list of bits

  • message – Message to authenticate

  • algorithm – Hash algorithm to use

Returns:

Hexadecimal representation of the MAC

static verify_message_authentication_code(key: list[int], message: bytes, mac: str, algorithm: str = 'sha256') → bool[source]

Verify a message authentication code.

Parameters:

  • key – Quantum key as a list of bits

  • message – Message to verify

  • mac – Message authentication code to verify

  • algorithm – Hash algorithm to use

Returns:

True if MAC is valid, False otherwise

class qkdpy.QuantumKeyValidation[source]

Validation mechanisms for quantum keys.

static statistical_randomness_test(key: list[int]) → dict[source]

Perform statistical randomness tests on a quantum key.

Parameters:

key – Quantum key as a list of bits

Returns:

Dictionary with test results

static entropy_test(key: list[int]) → float[source]

Calculate the entropy of a quantum key.

Parameters:

key – Quantum key as a list of bits

Returns:

Entropy value (0 to 1, where 1 is maximum entropy)

static correlation_test(key: list[int], lag: int = 1) → float[source]

Test for correlations in the key at a specific lag.

Parameters:

  • key – Quantum key as a list of bits

  • lag – Lag for correlation test

Returns:

Correlation coefficient (-1 to 1)

class qkdpy.QuantumSideChannelProtection[source]

Protection against side-channel attacks in QKD implementations.

static constant_time_compare(key1: list[int], key2: list[int]) → bool[source]

Compare two keys in constant time to prevent timing attacks.

Parameters:

  • key1 – First key

  • key2 – Second key

Returns:

True if keys are equal, False otherwise

static secure_key_splitting(key: list[int], num_parts: int) → list[list[int]][source]

Split a key into multiple parts using XOR secret sharing.

Parameters:

  • key – Key to split

  • num_parts – Number of parts to split into

Returns:

List of key parts

static reconstruct_key(parts: list[list[int]]) → list[int][source]

Reconstruct a key from its parts.

Parameters:

parts – List of key parts

Returns:

Reconstructed key

exception qkdpy.QKDException(message: str, *, error_code: str | None = None, context: dict[str, Any] | None = None, cause: Exception | None = None, recoverable: bool = True)[source]

Base exception for all QKDpy errors.

Provides structured error information including error codes, context data, and recovery suggestions for enterprise deployments.

__init__(message: str, *, error_code: str | None = None, context: dict[str, Any] | None = None, cause: Exception | None = None, recoverable: bool = True) → None[source]

Initialize QKD exception.

Parameters:

  • message – Human-readable error message

  • error_code – Machine-readable error code for logging

  • context – Additional context data for debugging

  • cause – Original exception that caused this error

  • recoverable – Whether the operation can be retried

error_code: str = 'QKD_GENERIC_ERROR'
to_dict() → dict[str, Any][source]

Convert exception to dictionary for logging/serialization.

Context values keyed by names in the sensitive-data denylist (e.g., raw_key, secret_key) are redacted to [REDACTED] before return so error reports do not leak secrets.

exception qkdpy.ProtocolError(message: str, *, error_code: str | None = None, context: dict[str, Any] | None = None, cause: Exception | None = None, recoverable: bool = True)[source]

Base class for protocol-related errors.

error_code: str = 'QKD_PROTOCOL_ERROR'
exception qkdpy.ProtocolSecurityError(message: str, *, qber: float | None = None, threshold: float | None = None, attack_type: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any)[source]

Security violation detected in protocol execution.

Raised when QBER exceeds threshold, eavesdropping is detected, or other security conditions are violated.

error_code: str = 'QKD_SECURITY_VIOLATION'
__init__(message: str, *, qber: float | None = None, threshold: float | None = None, attack_type: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any) → None[source]

Initialize QKD exception.

Parameters:

  • message – Human-readable error message

  • error_code – Machine-readable error code for logging

  • context – Additional context data for debugging

  • cause – Original exception that caused this error

  • recoverable – Whether the operation can be retried

Protocol is in an invalid state for the requested operation.

error_code: str = 'QKD_PROTOCOL_STATE_ERROR'

Insufficient key material generated.
